rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 11, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT HIDETO MATSUSHIMA DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must state facts that demonstrate diligence to discover defendant's true identity. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NANCY J YEE VS. EDWARD YEE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 11, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT JENNY GRANADOS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Continued to February 5, 2010. All parties to provide courtesy copies with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AZUCENA PEREZ et al VS. NICOLAS HARO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 11, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS NICOLAS HARO, RAMONA HARO Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Motion granted with leave to amend as to fifth and sixth causes of action. While a concealment cause of action does not require the same particularity as a misrepresentation cause of action, it appears that plaintiffs are attempting to plead both. The misrepresentation claims must be pleaded with more particularity. Motion granted with leave to amend as to the ninth cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. Plaintiff to seek emotional distress damages under the eighth cause of action. Motion granted without leave to amend as to the eleventh cause of action for nuisance as duplicative. Motion denied as to the remaining grounds. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BOREL PRIVATE BANK & TRUST COMPANY VS. BURT HAMROL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Receiver And Preliminary Injuction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 11, 2010, line 8, PLAINTIFF BOREL PRIVATE BANK & TRUST COMPANY Motion For Order Appointing Receiver and Preliminary Injuction. Motion for order appointing a receiver granted. Upon default, contract gives the receiver the power to take possession of all property, with the power to protect and preserve, to operate preceding a foreclosure or sale and collect rents. Defendant defaulted and does not presently have sufficient funds to resume development. Completion of the building appears proper to protect and preserve plaintiff's security interest in the building. Preliminary Injunction granted. Loans total over $8.7 million. Defendant's loans are secured by property that is only 50 % developed. If property remains idle, value will decrease if it remains in its current condition. Imminent irreparable harm demonstrated by increased risk of vandalism, weather damage and decrease in value due to stalled construction. Plaintiff is likely to prevail on the merits and the harm to plaintiff outweighs the harm to defendant. Hearing required to address the amounts of the bonds. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ARJUN DUTT et al VS. LAWRENCE DAGESSE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 11, 2010, line 13, PLAINTIFFS ARJUN DUTT, RITU VOHRA MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Motion to strike denied. The habitability of the premises will affect the fair market value and daily damages. The court cannot rule as a matter of law that the allegations are irrelevant, false, or improper within the meaning of CCP section 435. =(302/CWW)
